title: AWS Certified Solutions Architect - Associate
description: Scenario practice batch for Design Resilient Architectures.

questions.question: A company runs an application on EC2 instances and wants unhealthy instances replaced automatically with minimal operational effort. Which design should be used?
answers: A single EC2 instance with detailed monitoring
An Auto Scaling group across multiple Availability Zones
An EC2 instance with manual snapshots
A dedicated host in one AZ
questions.shortExplanation: An Auto Scaling group across multiple AZs provides both self-healing and resilience to a single-AZ failure.
questions.fullExplanation: An Auto Scaling group across multiple AZs provides both self-healing and resilience to a single-AZ failure.
questions.explanation: An Auto Scaling group across multiple AZs provides both self-healing and resilience to a single-AZ failure.

questions.question: A workload must continue if one Availability Zone fails. Which database deployment option best meets that requirement with minimal application changes?
questions.shortExplanation: RDS Multi-AZ offers managed synchronous standby and automated failover with minimal app changes.
questions.fullExplanation: RDS Multi-AZ offers managed synchronous standby and automated failover with minimal app changes.
questions.explanation: RDS Multi-AZ offers managed synchronous standby and automated failover with minimal app changes.

questions.question: A spike in orders can overwhelm a downstream order processor. Which service best decouples the application tiers and smooths bursts?
questions.shortExplanation: Amazon SQS buffers burst traffic so the downstream processor can catch up at a stable rate.
questions.fullExplanation: Amazon SQS buffers burst traffic so the downstream processor can catch up at a stable rate.
questions.explanation: Amazon SQS buffers burst traffic so the downstream processor can catch up at a stable rate.

questions.question: A company needs DNS-based failover from a primary Region to a disaster recovery Region. Which service should control the failover?
answers: Amazon Route 53 health checks and failover routing
questions.shortExplanation: Route 53 failover routing is built for health-check-driven DNS redirection between endpoints or Regions.
questions.fullExplanation: Route 53 failover routing is built for health-check-driven DNS redirection between endpoints or Regions.
questions.explanation: Route 53 failover routing is built for health-check-driven DNS redirection between endpoints or Regions.

questions.question: An architect wants to reduce duplicate order processing caused by retries from an event source. What application pattern should be added?
questions.shortExplanation: Idempotent processing avoids duplicate business actions when the same message is retried or replayed.
questions.fullExplanation: Idempotent processing avoids duplicate business actions when the same message is retried or replayed.
questions.explanation: Idempotent processing avoids duplicate business actions when the same message is retried or replayed.

questions.question: A team needs object storage for backups with very high durability and native cross-Region replication options. Which service is the best fit?
questions.shortExplanation: Amazon S3 provides high durability, lifecycle controls, and built-in replication features suitable for resilient backups.
questions.fullExplanation: Amazon S3 provides high durability, lifecycle controls, and built-in replication features suitable for resilient backups.
questions.explanation: Amazon S3 provides high durability, lifecycle controls, and built-in replication features suitable for resilient backups.

questions.question: A system must recover in another Region within hours while keeping cost lower than active-active. Which disaster recovery strategy is usually the best fit?
questions.shortExplanation: Pilot light balances cost and recovery speed by keeping the most critical components pre-positioned.
questions.fullExplanation: Pilot light balances cost and recovery speed by keeping the most critical components pre-positioned.
questions.explanation: Pilot light balances cost and recovery speed by keeping the most critical components pre-positioned.

questions.question: Which design choice most reduces cascading failure between microservices?
answers: Hard-coded synchronous dependencies for every call
One large database transaction across all services
Keeping all services on one instance
questions.shortExplanation: Loose coupling and asynchronous messaging reduce shared failure paths and simplify independent recovery.
questions.fullExplanation: Loose coupling and asynchronous messaging reduce shared failure paths and simplify independent recovery.
questions.explanation: Loose coupling and asynchronous messaging reduce shared failure paths and simplify independent recovery.

questions.question: A database serves many read-heavy queries and the primary is becoming saturated. Which option improves resilience and read scalability?
answers: Move the database into a public subnet
questions.shortExplanation: Read replicas offload read traffic from the writer and can improve both scale and failure isolation.
questions.fullExplanation: Read replicas offload read traffic from the writer and can improve both scale and failure isolation.
questions.explanation: Read replicas offload read traffic from the writer and can improve both scale and failure isolation.

questions.question: A team has backups but has never attempted a restore. What is the biggest risk?
answers: The backups may fail when needed
The instances will scale too fast
questions.shortExplanation: Backup validity is unproven until restores are tested, and many real failures surface only during recovery.
questions.fullExplanation: Backup validity is unproven until restores are tested, and many real failures surface only during recovery.
questions.explanation: Backup validity is unproven until restores are tested, and many real failures surface only during recovery.

questions.question: A queue consumer repeatedly fails on the same malformed message and healthy messages are piling up behind it. What should be added?
questions.shortExplanation: A dead-letter queue removes poison messages from the main flow so healthy processing can continue.
questions.fullExplanation: A dead-letter queue removes poison messages from the main flow so healthy processing can continue.
questions.explanation: A dead-letter queue removes poison messages from the main flow so healthy processing can continue.

questions.question: A company wants its application to keep serving read-only requests if a noncritical write dependency fails. Which design principle is most relevant?
questions.shortExplanation: Graceful degradation preserves core user value by temporarily disabling noncritical functionality during partial failure.
questions.fullExplanation: Graceful degradation preserves core user value by temporarily disabling noncritical functionality during partial failure.
questions.explanation: Graceful degradation preserves core user value by temporarily disabling noncritical functionality during partial failure.

questions.question: An architecture spans multiple tiers and the team wants to minimize the impact of one service failure. Which principle best applies?
answers: Use the root account for automation
questions.shortExplanation: Deliberate failure isolation keeps one tier or dependency problem from propagating across the whole system.
questions.fullExplanation: Deliberate failure isolation keeps one tier or dependency problem from propagating across the whole system.
questions.explanation: Deliberate failure isolation keeps one tier or dependency problem from propagating across the whole system.

questions.question: A company needs near-zero data loss but can accept a longer recovery time in another Region. Which design concern is the highest priority?
questions.shortExplanation: When near-zero data loss is required, the design must prioritize replication patterns that minimize RPO.
questions.fullExplanation: When near-zero data loss is required, the design must prioritize replication patterns that minimize RPO.
questions.explanation: When near-zero data loss is required, the design must prioritize replication patterns that minimize RPO.

questions.question: A health check continually reports failed targets but traffic is still being sent to them. What is most likely missing?
answers: A control action tied to the health check result
questions.shortExplanation: Health checks only improve resilience when they trigger routing, scaling, or recovery actions automatically.
questions.fullExplanation: Health checks only improve resilience when they trigger routing, scaling, or recovery actions automatically.
questions.explanation: Health checks only improve resilience when they trigger routing, scaling, or recovery actions automatically.
